strengths: Degree taxonomy now aligns cleanly with both ResearchPlan.md and real university training patterns.
Undergraduate curriculum is structured as a realistic progression from foundations to proof transition to pure/applied/statistical depth.
Graduate section now captures not only subfields but also the actual mechanics of research training: quals, seminars, reading courses, advisor selection, and dissertation work.
Textbook guidance is now course-by-course and level-aware, with standard, honors, self-study, and graduate continuation options.
Canonical literature section is concrete, broad, and much better balanced across pure math, applied math, probability/statistics, combinatorics, and logic.
The file now functions as a genuine research-entry roadmap rather than only a high-level overview.
remaining_gaps: A few subfields could still be expanded further if the repository later wants a more exhaustive branch-by-branch bibliography, especially in statistics, ergodic theory, and representation theory.
The reading list is intentionally curated rather than exhaustive, so advanced specialists would still need a second-layer subfield bibliography.
Methods and tools are strong, but branch-specific datasets/workflows are naturally thinner in mathematics than in empirical disciplines.
01_mathematics.summary: The revised Mathematics.md now satisfies the core standard of Requirement.md: it presents a full educational pathway, concrete methods and tools, strong course-by-course textbook recommendations, a substantial and field-appropriate literature roadmap, and a realistic picture of how students become research-capable mathematicians.
